title: To The Honoured Master Endymion Porter.
content:
When to thy porch I come and ravish'd see
    The state of poets there attending thee,
    Those bards and I, all in a chorus sing:
    We are thy prophets, Porter, thou our king.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To The Honoured Master Endymion Porter.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
